name: Gainpath Loop 2 — Short-Term Fixes from Architecture Review
description: Actionable backlog derived from US-005 architecture review and US-001 security review findings. Targets the highest-impact short-term fixes across input validation, CORS safety, offline sync, AI client efficiency, and frontend error handling.

userStories.title: Add field-level validation and status transition guards to Pydantic schemas
userStories.description: Multiple endpoints spread payload.model_dump() into setattr() loops without range validation, allowed-value constraints, or immutable-field protection. Add explicit constraints to SessionLogUpdate (allowed status transitions, immutable id), SessionLogCreate (session_type enum), and UserProfileUpdate. Add a status transition validator so you can't go from 'completed' back to 'planned'.
acceptanceCriteria: SessionLogUpdate.status uses Literal['planned','in_progress','completed','skipped'] or an enum, not Optional[str].
SessionLogCreate.session_type uses an enum or Literal type with allowed values.
A status transition validator prevents invalid transitions (e.g. completed → planned).
The 'id' field is excluded from all setattr() update loops.
Existing tests still pass or are updated to match new validation.

userStories.title: Validate CORS origins at startup — reject wildcard in production
userStories.description: Currently cors_origins defaults to localhost and if misconfigured to ['*'] in production, the API accepts calls from any origin. Since auth is HTTP Basic, this would leak credentials. Add startup validation that rejects ['*'] when APP_ENV != 'development'.
acceptanceCriteria: Startup validation in config.py rejects CORS_ORIGINS containing '*' when APP_ENV is not 'development'.
A clear error message explains why wildcard CORS is forbidden in production.
Development mode still allows any CORS configuration for local ergonomics.

userStories.title: Add Pydantic models for offline sync payloads and validate before ORM insertion
userStories.description: The offline sync endpoint's _apply_action spreads arbitrary payload.items() directly into ORM models. Add explicit Pydantic models (CreateSetLogPayload, CompleteSessionPayload, AddBodyFeedbackPayload) and validate incoming payloads against them before touching the database. Exclude the 'id' field from complete_session setattr loop.
acceptanceCriteria: Each offline action type has a dedicated Pydantic model that validates payload shape.
_apply_action validates payloads against the correct model before ORM insertion.
The 'id' field is excluded from the complete_session setattr loop.
Invalid or unknown payload keys are rejected with a 422 response.

userStories.title: Reuse httpx.AsyncClient across AI calls instead of creating per request
userStories.description: AIClient._http_post creates a new httpx.AsyncClient per call, discarding it after one request. This means TCP+TLS setup happens per AI call. Create the client once in __init__ or as a managed singleton, reuse it across calls, and add a close() method for graceful shutdown.
acceptanceCriteria: httpx.AsyncClient is created once in AIClient.__init__ and reused across calls.
A close() or shutdown method is available for graceful cleanup.
The client uses appropriate timeout and connection pool limits.
AI client tests pass or are updated for the new lifecycle.

userStories.title: Fix coaching chat orphan message bug
userStories.description: coaching_engine.chat() stores the user message and commits before calling the AI model. If the AI call fails, the user message is committed but has no assistant row. The except block stores a fallback string so it's not catastrophic, but the two-message chat contract is fragile. Store both messages in a single transaction: generate the AI response first, then commit user + assistant together.
acceptanceCriteria: User and assistant messages are committed together in a single db.commit() when AI succeeds.
On AI failure, the fallback assistant message is still stored alongside the user message in the same commit.
No orphan user-message rows are possible after any code path.
Existing coaching endpoint behavior is preserved.

userStories.title: Add Content-Security-Policy and security headers to proxy responses
userStories.description: The Next.js proxy route strips WWW-Authenticate but doesn't add any security headers. Add Content-Security-Policy, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, and Referrer-Policy headers to all proxied responses to harden against XSS and clickjacking.
acceptanceCriteria: Proxy route adds Content-Security-Policy header with sensible defaults (script-src, style-src, etc.).
X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff is set on all proxy responses.
X-Frame-Options: DENY or SAMEORIGIN is set.
Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin is set.
Headers do not break normal app functionality (API calls, AI coaching, offline sync).

userStories.title: Add frontend error state handling and error boundary convention
userStories.description: useQuery calls handle loading with skeleton cards but have no error state rendering. useMutation hooks have no onError callback. Add error state rendering to critical query hooks (sessions, coaching, dashboard) with retry buttons or toast notifications. Add an ErrorBoundary component wrapping the app.
acceptanceCriteria: Critical useQuery hooks in TodayView and ActiveWorkout render an error state when the API fails.
useMutation hooks for session complete/skip have onError callbacks with user-visible feedback (toast or inline).
An ErrorBoundary component catches render crashes and shows a recovery UI.
Error states preserve the existing visual language.

userStories.title: Migrate FastAPI startup/shutdown to lifespan context manager
userStories.description: The scheduler uses the deprecated @app.on_event('startup') pattern and has no graceful shutdown. Migrate to the lifespan context manager pattern, storing the scheduler instance and calling scheduler.shutdown() on exit. This also enables proper httpx client cleanup from US-104.
acceptanceCriteria: The deprecated on_event('startup') is replaced with a lifespan context manager.
The AsyncIOScheduler is started in lifespan startup and shut down in lifespan shutdown.
The lifespan also manages the AIClient httpx client lifecycle if applicable.
App behavior is unchanged — scheduler runs the same jobs.
Graceful shutdown no longer hangs.

userStories.title: Replace datetime.utcnow() with datetime.now(timezone.utc) across all files
userStories.description: Multiple files use datetime.utcnow() which is deprecated in Python 3.12+. Replace all instances with datetime.now(timezone.utc). Optionally create a shared now_utc() helper.
acceptanceCriteria: No remaining calls to datetime.utcnow() in the backend codebase.
All replacements use datetime.now(timezone.utc) or a shared now_utc() helper.
A shared utility function exists if more than 3 files needed the change.
Backend tests pass or are updated.

userStories.title: Replace hardcoded PaceForge branding with settings.app_name
userStories.description: The AI client X-Title header and some backend references are hardcoded to 'PaceForge'. Replace them with settings.app_name so the product name can be changed from config. Also update the localStorage key prefix if practical.
acceptanceCriteria: AI client X-Title header uses settings.app_name instead of hardcoded 'PaceForge'.
Backend health endpoint app name uses settings.app_name.
Frontend localStorage key is documented as a known migration item if changed.
All existing tests pass or are updated.
